study_group_representative.quote: We discuss COVID-19 control approaches in selected African countries and the need for shared, affordable, innovative methods to overcome challenges and minimize mortality.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: In South Africa, approximately 28 000 community healthcare workers (CHCWs) have been deployed. By the end of April 2020, more than 6 million people had been screened and 42 000 referred for testing.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: Sixteen percent of those screened were referred for testing at health facilities (range, 4%– 48% by subdistrict).

follow_up_length_reported.quote: As of 26 May 2020, the World Health Organization Africa Region (WHO-AFRO) had reported 80 979 coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) cases with 2193 deaths from 45 countries...

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: In South Africa, approximately 28 000 community healthcare workers (CHCWs) have been deployed.

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: Figure 1 shows COVID-19 screening activity by subdistrict in Cape Town, the epicenter of the pandemic in South Africa. Sixteen percent of those screened were referred for testing at health facilities (range, 4%– 48% by subdistrict).

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: Table 1 describes the implementation challenges and possible solutions.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: We discuss COVID-19 screening, testing, and contact tracing experiences from selected African countries and the need for shared, affordable approaches and innovations to overcome challenges and minimize mortality rates.
